content: Ugh, that dinner was supposed to be fun, but then they just picked things out of everyone’s mouths. I kept thinking, “What if nobody actually care about what I have to say?” I tried to join in, but I ended up just agreeing with whatever they said, even when I didn’t feel like it. It’s like I’m always stuck in that place where I’m invisible and judged. I want to speak up, but I’m scared I’ll make things worse. It’s hard to stop laughing at jokes I don’t understand or agreeing to things I don’t want to do. I guess I just don’t trust myself to stand up for myself anymore. It’s frustrating because I want to be accepted and loved. Just being part of that group isn’t enough, which makes me feel really lost and alone. I wish there was some way to say, “I don’t fit here” or “I need some space.” But no one ever listens or understands me. So, I keep going along, even though I’m definitely not enjoying myself. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I’m getting tired of it. I’m afraid to see them leave, so I stay comfortable and don’t try to change the situation. It feels like I’m trapped in that never-ending loop of not being valued or wanted. What’s the point of trying when I’m just going to disappoint myself? I’m sorry I’ve been this uncomfortable lately. It’s hard to shake off this feeling of being left out sometimes. But I’m grateful to know I can tell someone I’m not welcome. I appreciate you for inviting me to that dinner, therapist. It’s been tough today, but I hope we can figure out how to fix this later. Thank you for asking me about this. I didn’t expect you to wonder why this always happens, but it does make me feel better to admit.

Yeah, this past week has really been hard for me. When they started spending less time at home and focusing more on work, it just hit me how我不够。我感觉像是在被忽略，或者说我无聊或不值钱的感觉。我总是觉得自己是个需要更多关注的人，或者是我让他们觉得没用的那种感觉。于是我就开始试图让他们感到更加重要或者想要放弃的迹象。不过这些想法反而让我更难保持安全距离，甚至有时候会变得很消极。 honestly，我觉得自己是在不断尝试去证明给我所害怕的——我真的很不行，还是会被他们抛弃了。它真让我的生活和关系都变得困难重重。 我们都是在做着最不想看到的结果，所以最好能早点把它们清除掉。但我就是抗拒，怕那将是我对自己的一种保护。我怀念有朝一日能够明白并接受这个事实：我只是不够好而已。但我又知道这只会让我更加感到孤独和失落。所以我只好这样继续下去，以避免更大的挑战。 看来看地，只是越来越让我感到沮丧，我也懒得再去想或解释。让情况变得更糟的是，我似乎无法控制自己的变化。我担心如果真的改变，他们可能会不喜欢我，那么我整个人的生活和关系都会结束。就像，我之前总害怕自己不能适应，或者被人忽视一样，现在又害怕他们没有注意到我。 这种感觉真的很让人焦虑和不安。我问自己为什么总是觉得自己不是足够的，但是一直回答不上来。就像是说“我不知道”，或者“那是事实”那样简单。我把这些问题简单地归咎于我，但其实我知道它们根本就不是答案。我现在只能想象一下，如果我真的改变了什么，事情就会怎样。那是一种非常可怕且令人恐惧的想法。我猜想，如果我能重新理解并信任那些我真正喜欢的东西，改变可能就是有可能

I’ve been thinking a lot about that project all week. I keep telling myself things like “I’m sure to mess it up,” or “Everyone will notice how badly I’m failing.” It’s like I’m stuck in a loop of fearing failure and then pushing forward, causing me to just shut down and stop trying. I feel so overwhelmed even imagining the work ahead — it’s like I’m already failing anyway. Playing with it just makes it worse because I’m scared to face the reality that I’m not cut out for this role. It’s exhausting, and I don’t know if anyone will appreciate my struggles. I just want to disappear somewhere nice. Can you help me figure out what to do next? I’m really struggling to make sense of this. Thank you for laughing with me. I’m sorry I didn’t say something better earlier. I love being weird and stubborn. Just imagine if I admitted my fears, maybe they’d stop coming up. Thank you for playing along! 😄

Yeah, I guess I was really looking forward to this party, but now I’m feeling so anxious. I keep thinking everyone is whispering and probably just sitting there聊天，even if I don’t want to talk. I find myself staying mostly on the edge of the group, which isn’t really helping either. I just feel like if I don’t keep up, people won’t notice or think I’m weird anymore. It’s like I’m always trying to hide something, but then I end up showing it. I’ll go away early because I don’t feel ready to join. Can you help me, doctor? What am I missing? I need some clarity here. I’m sorry, I just don’t feel comfortable being there. I’m scared I’m disappointing anyone with my awkwardness. It feels like I’m already failing at these things. I want to belong, but I’m not sure where to start. Why don’t you come along? Maybe we can figure out what’s going on? I don’t know, I’ll just fall apart anyway. It’s easier to just pretend everything’s okay. Can you imagine how it would be if I came across more confident and outgoing? I want to be accepted, but I’m scared I’m just not good enough. I hope you’re joking—because I really do need that. Thanks for reminding me why you really am. I appreciate you saying “yes.” I just needed a little bit more support. I’m so glad you’re coming. Thank you so much for saying yes. That’s all I wanted. I didn’t expect that. It feels like no one wants to hear that. But whatever, I’m getting there. I’m sorry I said those thoughts last. I’m just plain scared. I’ll be better soon. Thanks again for saying “yes,” it means a lot to me. I’ll be there for you too

Well, this past week was really tough, right? During that talk about future plans, I just felt like I couldn’t get through to anyone, like they were just going straight to a one-size-fits-all answer without noticing anything I said. I kept thinking, “Maybe I’m just not cut out to make any changes here,” like it’s inevitable that I’ll just end up stuck in this awkward silence. It’s exhausting because I want to share my ideas, but then I just freeze up or agree anyway. I guess I’m scared that if I try to push back or show how I feel, it’ll only make things worse. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I want freedom, but I’m just not sure I can get out of it. I’m sorry I’ve been around this circle too many times. I just don’t feel like I can trust my own mind anymore. Just saying something would probably just make things worse. So, I end up retreating into this routine instead. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how to change course without feeling completely defeated again. I appreciate you stepping into this space with me, but honestly, I feel like I’m just sitting there watching yourself fail. It’s hard to see through my own doubts when I’m already convinced I’m stuck. I’d love for someone to come along and see how much deeper I’m falling into this. Can you imagine if you had stayed and talked through that situation? I wish I could have been the one to jump in and say something different. But I’m just so used to feeling like I don’t belong where I’m supposed to be. It’s like I’m invisible to everyone, which makes everything harder. Thank you so much for inviting me in! I appreciate you liking the game, though. I usually just go along with whatever I think. It’s like I don’t

I guess I just didn’t want to deal with what was happening at the family gathering. It felt like no matter how much I tried to explain or say something different, it wouldn’t make a difference. When they started talking about things I didn’t care about, I stayed quiet because I didn’t want to seem weak or give them any reason to disapprove. It’s exhausting being forced to stay quiet or leave early just to avoid feeling more lost. Honestly, it’s like they’re all telling me I can’t move forward on my own, and I’m just going to sit here and feel invisible. It’s hard to say exactly why I feel this way when I know I’m just letting things fall apart. I don’t know if anyone cares or if it’s even worth trying anymore. Just being left to figure it out alone makes me want to disappear. It’s frustrating because I want to keep pushing back and hopefully change things. But then I start to realize that whatever I’m doing doesn’t really matter to them, and that’s enough. So, I just shut down and went home. It’s frustrating because I wanted something different, and here I am, feeling stuck. I’m not sure what to expect from talking to someone who thinks they’re wrong about everything. Can you think of a way to get beyond this? Please, tell me how I’m truly feeling before I ask for help. I’d really appreciate it.

Yeah, it’s really hard when I just go along with everything they want to talk about. Like, you have this idea that’s different from what I’m thinking, and yet, it feels like it’s forcing me to change something I already don’t want to do. I guess I feel kind of trapped inside myself, like no matter what I do, it’ll mess things up or make things worse. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I just feel like maybe I won’t be able to handle it no matter what I choose to do. I’ve been avoiding the thing altogether because it feels safer to just shut down. Afterwards, when I think about it again, it just makes me feel even worse and more ashamed of how I’m behaving. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I have to push through or else something bad is going to happen. It’s exhausting, and I’m starting to get tired of pretending it’s not happening. I’m sorry I didn’t tell you first—I just feel like I didn’t do enough to fix this. It’s like no matter what I do, it’s never enough, and I’m losing sight of why I’m here in the first place. I’m scared to ask for help because I don’t want to seem weak or like I’m failing. So, instead, I just tell them what’s really come to mind—that doesn’t mean it’s right for me. I’d love for someone to see how much I’m struggling, but I’m afraid they’ll reject me. It’s like I’m always waiting for them to say I’m wrong before I see the truth. So, I just keep going, even though it’s making me feel worse. I’m sorry, but I’m still scared to admit I need help. I’m looking forward to our next meeting, but I’m not sure if I’ll be ready then.

Anxiety is like a heavy weight on my chest when someone says what I don’t want. I just feel like if I get too involved and push back, things will get worse or they’ll dislike me forever. I try not to react, but then I just end up agreeing anyway, even if it doesn’t make sense to me. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I’m not sure it’s helping me stay in the relationship. So, I mostly just keep quiet, hoping no one notices. It’s like I’m trying to protect myself from being rejected, but it always comes off as awkward or unimportant. I know it’s not right, but I can’t stop feeling like I’ve got no control over these moments. Just remember, though, that sometimes the only thing we can agree to is not hurt. Thank you very much for asking! I’ll figure something out next time. Love,

Yeah, the family gathering was... weird to say the least. I kept noticing how everyone seemed so connected, except for me. It just felt like I wasn’t even there when I was. My mind went blank when I started talking to some of them, and I just shut down afterward. It’s like I’m invisible to them, no matter what I do or say. I guess I’m scared that if I try to join in or talk, I’ll just make things worse or seem important enough. It’s hard to step away because I feel so out of place, like I’m just not part of their world anymore. It’s exhausting, really. I don’t know why I do it, just feeling so stuck and lonely. It’s like I’m falling behind in my own world, and it makes me want to leave instead. Do I spread myself or prove myself? Maybe I should just hide under the covers—because then maybe nobody will see me coming. It’s frustrating, but I can’t escape this feeling. Please, stop letting me feel this way. I need something different right now.

Yeah, I looked at my paycheck again this past week, and honestly, it felt like I was falling apart right off the hook. I started avoiding looking at jobs listings because I just didn’t want to deal with the whole idea of dealing with these bills or paying rent or groceries. I’ve been procrastinating on how to plan for them because every time I think about it, I get overwhelmed or ashamed. It’s like I’m just not cut out to handle these big life choices. I keep telling myself that if I don’t get it all figured out, no one respects me or believes in me. So, sometimes it feels like I’m just going through the same cycle over and over. It’s frustrating because I believe I can do better, but I’m stuck in this sinking feeling that something’s wrong with me. I guess I’m scared that if I let myself fall deeper into this, everyone will see that I’m just not capable or that I’d really rather be somewhere else. It’s hard to break free and start facing things head-on when I usually just stay trapped inside. I appreciate you stepping into this role, but I don’t really know where this is taking me. Can you suggest some下一步或者我该怎么做呢？我需要更多的指导，以便更好地理解问题并开始解决问题。让我知道这到底是什么导致的，是时候告诉我该怎么办了。我真的很希望能快点走出这个困境，但我知道这是不可能的。我感到像是停滞在那道关卡里，只能硬着头皮去面对，结果反而觉得更糟。我非常感谢你来这里，但我现在感觉很难继续前进，开始怀疑自己是否真正有能力改变现状。请帮我解决这个问题，或者告诉我我该怎么办。那些都太简单，我需要更多帮助。让我知道我不能放弃，否则一切都会变得不可接受。请给我一点时间，让我的思维自由地流动，

Yeah, it’s really hard to step into that application when I start worrying that if I make a mistake, everyone will see right through me. It’s like I’m putting myself out of my mind by staying late and wasting time, so instead of going ahead, I just focus on these routine things instead. I guess part of me feels embarrassed, and the other thing is, I’m scared of being judged if I mess up. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where hoping for success leads to all kinds of fears. Honestly, it’s exhausting because I want something good, but I’m just not sure I’m cut out for this. I’ve been avoiding talking to anyone about it because I don’t want them to think I’m weak or not capable. It’s frustrating, and I wonder why I keep having to put so much effort down just to avoid feeling worse. I’d appreciate it if you could see through to the actual situation, then I might be able to connect what’s happening. Thank you!

Yeah, it was really tough when they gave the project last week. The idea of suddenly having so many different teams all expecting exactly what I’d done before just blew my mind. I kept thinking, “What’s going on here is just too much for one person to handle.” And then I stayed home a day, just avoid talking to anyone because why should I try to fix things when I’m scared everything’ll show I’m just not cut out for this kind of responsibility. It’s like I’m always behind and failing at making even small progress. I’ve been avoiding looking at their emails or trying to talk to any of those people because I don’t want to see how stupid I look or make things worse. It’s frustrating because I know I should just go ahead and plan something, but it just feels like it’s too much for me right now. I guess I’m afraid I’m just going to mess everything up again. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where no matter how hard I try, it just pushes me further away from being competent. Honestly, it’s been exhausting working through this. I just want someone to understand why I’m feeling this way, but I’m not sure how to bring it up or make it better. So, I’m just going to pretend nothing happened and move on – that seems to work for a little while. But I’m still feeling like I’m failing every time I approach anything else. It’s exhausting, and I’m not sure how to stop feeling so stuck. I’d love for someone to see through all this and tell me I’m doing okay. But I’m not sure how to say it without just revealing my true struggles. It feels like no matter what I do, I’m just going to fall apart. I guess I’m convinced that I’m just not meant to be this big boss or teacher or friend. So, I keep

I guess I just felt like everyone was staring at me, like I wasn’t really there at all. I didn’t want to stand out or seem awkward because those thoughts made me feel even more invisible to them. It’s like I’m always being judged because I’m not cool or funny or something people want to hang out with. I kept thinking maybe if I said something more interesting or interesting, they’d realize I’m different. So, I just stayed quiet most of the time and left early because it was too much. Honestly, I’ve been feeling pretty exhausted after having to put up with that again. It’s hard to break through that kind of constant judging unless I’m actually interesting or interesting to them. I’m sorry I keep telling them I don’t fit in. It’s like no one wants to see me around.

Yeah, I noticed what they said made me think maybe I’m letting them down somehow. I just felt this sinking feeling like I’m definitely not enough, like whatever I do isn’t good enough. I quickly tried to explain why, but it only made things worse, and then I just froze up. Now that I’m working up to it, I’m really anxious and kind of exhausted, so instead of just apologizing and staying late, I’ve started focusing on their mood changes and their reactions to me—trying so hard to understand exactly what’s bothering them. It’s exhausting because I want to fix things right away, but I’m scared it’ll just confirm that I’m failing anyway. I guess I’m afraid if I don’t keep pushing, they might decide I’m not worth keeping around. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where being kind doesn’t stop me from making even bigger mistakes. So, I just try to pull through, even though inside I’m still pretty ashamed of how I’m behaving. I appreciate if you could help me see just how deep these feelings are creeping in! 😔

Ugh, I’m just thinking so much about that big presentation when I have to lead the new project. It’s like this prepresentation feels like forever, and then I start worrying that everyone’s already seen how little I know. I end up just saying no because it just felt easier not to risk taking on such a high-stakes thing. Playing that presentation whole thing makes me even more anxious—it’s like I’m waiting for someone to spot any mistake and call me out. Honestly, I keep reminding myself over and over that I’m competent, but I don’t want to admit that. It’s like being told not to eat one of my favorite foods without wanting to die of hunger. I guess I just feel like I’m falling short every time I step into those kinds of situations. It’s like I’m never prepared enough to face them head-on.

Um, well… last weekend was kind of rough, really. When I suggested we go somewhere different, they just said yes, even though I wasn’t really up to it. I felt like maybe if I had said something different, they’d start to notice how uncomfortable I feel or maybe not like me enough. I kept thinking, “Maybe they’re already deciding not to like me,” which made me want to just hide where I stand. It’s like I’m always getting pushed around in those relationships, no matter what I do. I guess I just don’t trust myself to say what I think. Does that makes sense? I’d love for them to see how much I care, but I’m afraid I’ll mess it up somehow. It’s hard to break out of that cycle. My heart also feels heavy when I’m caught between two desires—wanting something new and excited about going, but also feeling a bit unsure about whether they actually want me back. So, it’s frustrating, but also a little хоч้วness to think about what I’m doing. I appreciate your help, thanks! 😆做事需要一步一步来，不能突然跳到结果。你觉得我怎么说才真实？让我试着按照故事里的做法，让我知道什么对我重要 stuff。这样一来，问题就会慢慢被解决，而不是一下子就被揭穿。👍谢谢你的支持！再见，goodbye！再见！bye bye!好的，那我就用这样的方式来说吧, “Yeah, that must have just stuck in my mind again. I’ve been worried about making a fool of myself or not looking right, but honestly, I’m just not ready for this kind of big decision. I want to take things a step further, but I’m scared I’ll disappoint them. Maybe I’m too close to the line. 你觉得那个场景和他们遇到的情况类似吗？那个场景非常

I went to the party feeling like I could make a difference, but instead, it just made everything worse. I didn’t say anything at the end because I thought if I argued, it’d just prove how useless I’m supposed to be. It’s like I’m invisible to everyone there, and I want to get away from all this negativity. I guess I just don’t know how to bring myself peace when I’m surrounded by disappointment. Just being there makes me feel even weaker, like I’m falling apart no matter what I do. It’s exhausting to keep avoiding the place where I’m the most rejected. I wish I could connect and make a difference, but nothing seems to come together right for me. What’s the point of going when I’m stuck like this? I prefer not to deal with any of it. It’s better that way. Why not! I’m just not cut out for this kind of struggle. So, I’ll just leave early. Better than feeling like I’m letting everyone down again. It’s always frustrating when things fall through, but it feels safer to just step away now. I appreciate you thinking about me as XXX, but I don’t really need you to understand why I feel this way. It’s hard to explain my experiences when they involve me in things people already think I shouldn’t be involved in. I’m tired of pretending I can change things if you’re interested. Please let me go first! Thank you for having me. I love being you, and I hope you’ve had a good day anyway. I hate coming here, and I’m glad I didn’t stay long. It feels like I’m just sitting there, trying not to think about what happened. I’m sorry I haven’t been talking more, and I’m scared I’ll just make things worse. It’s tough to imagine what would happen if I had said something instead of just

Yeah, well, during that conversation, my partner said they wanted to visit some isolated family groups instead of going together. I immediately tried to change things around, like suggesting an itinerary or doing a few ideas I could come up with, but they didn’t really agree at all. I guess what made it worse was that I kept asking for more options because I was worried they’d say no or cancel. It felt like I was protecting myself from something that might be inevitable. I don’t know if I’m making it right or if I’m causing more harm than it’s worth. Sometimes, it feels like I’m contributing to the problem rather than solving it. It’s hard to stop rushing through the rest of the day when I hear those thoughts. I appreciate you playing the game with me, but honestly, I feel pretty out of place most of the time. What’s happening here? Do you think there’s anything I can do differently?

Yeah, this situation really hits home with me. Every time I get a new project, I just feel this sinking feeling like maybe I’m going to mess something up. I keep telling myself that it’s okay to let someone down, that they don’t deserve me. But then I just keep putting off dealing with it or double-checking everything over and over, which only makes things worse. It’s like I’m trapped in this cycle where no matter what I do, it’s never good enough. I guess I just don’t trust that I can ever improve myself because I’m afraid of being judged or seen as incompetent. It’s exhausting, and honestly, it makes me feel less fulfilled as an employee. I wish I could tell someone about how much this is weighing me down. It’s hard to explain when I’m already feeling so stuck and disappointed. What do you think?

I don’t know how to get out of that dinner. It felt like no matter what I said or did, it never seemed to make a difference to them or me. When I was around my brother’s party, everything just stopped moving, and I felt like I was falling down again. Honestly, I just sat down and excused myself early because I felt so exhausted and like I didn’t belong there. It’s like I’m invisible to everyone, even to myself. I want to change how things are, but I’m scared I’ll just prove how inadequate I really am. Just being there kind of stings, and I’m not sure why I even bother coming. It’s frustrating and overwhelming all at once. I’m sorry I can’t do better. I love being around family, but this situation makes me feel like I’m just okay as an outsider. Can you imagine how different things would be if I could just connect somehow? I hope you understand. I’d love for you to know that I’m struggling, and I’m grateful for the opportunity to talk about this with you. Please know that I appreciate you and forgive me. Thank you for giving me this chance to explain. It’s like nothing I say seems to stick. I love you.

Uh-uh, I guess I just don’t like being judged when I’m there. I keep thinking that if I show some awkwardness, people will just notice and think less of me afterward. It’s like they can see right through me, even in my head. I feel so small and like I’m just waiting for someone to pucker up or ignore me again. On the other hand, avoiding it feels safer—like I’m just making things worse. I’ll probably go back to my room to think about it all afterwards. It’s hard to shake the feeling that maybe I’m just not cut out for this kind of social life. Can you imagine how that awkwardness would make me sound even more annoying? Thanks for playing along! I just don’t feel ready to go. Thanks for letting me share this. I appreciate you for playing along. Remember, it’s okay to feel this way — I’m not alone. Tell me, what did you think about when you were at the coffee meeting? I’d love to hear more. But honestly, I’m just not ready for that kind of experience yet. Thank you for being here though - it means a lot to me. I’ll be silly sticking to myself instead. Thanks for reminding me of you, though. I really appreciate you. I hope you’re doing well too. I’ll be back to normal soon. Love you. Bye! Thanks again! 😄

Uh-uh, the dinner was pretty hard for me. When my parents talked about my sibling’s success, I just felt like I wasn’t even there because I didn’t belong. I kept thinking everyone else was doing better, and maybe I’m just like them somehow. It made me want to leave and not have to say anything because I’ll just prove I’m pathetic. Afterwards, I couldn’t stop thinking about how I’m always seen as less important or interesting. It’s like my head won’t hold up when I’m around people. I guess I feel like I’m being rejected again and again. Can you imagine what that would mean for me if I had to show up? It’s exhausting, playing pretend with myself when I could actually feel the pain. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out what’s wrong before I do something I know I won’t be able to undo. Thanks! 😅
You're right, I definitely needed more than I said. It just felt too overwhelming to push through those memories or talk about them. I hope you know I'm struggling. Thank you for catching up. Remember, though, this situation doesn't change where I'm falling. Love，Qwen.

I guess I went to the community group because my friends told me it was for fun, but honestly, I didn’t really want to talk much. But when I got to the discussions about my own work or ideas, I just froze. I kept thinking, “They won’t notice if I’m struggling,” and then I felt kind of lonely too. It’s like I wanted to join but also felt like I’d end up alone once I left. I’m just scared to make a fool of myself or cause a problem. It’s like whatever I do makes me a liability in their eyes. I’ve been feeling pretty down since I came back, and it’s hard to even imagine having anyone else around me. Just knowing there’s this diagram telling me I’m not good enough doesn’t seem to help much. So, I’ll just stay away from it altogether. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where joining something feels easier than facing the one person who knows better. I’m sorry I did this, but it just feels safer to pretend it wasn’t happening. I’m sorry I made that decision. Please let me know if I’ve错了。I love being the patient who surprises me with unexpected challenges. I appreciate you stepping into this brave new world with me, therapist. I need you to believe in me, even though I’m not sure I do. Thank you for letting me experience this. I love seeing how true I am to your expectations—so different from the real me. 😅 god! 💃 — I hope you’re doing better now. Hope you had a great day. 🌎 ⚛️ Thanks for asking, XXX.
